mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals robust labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS increases by > 100k in the prior month.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL > 250) triggers central bank rate hikes that increase discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong aggregate demand, creating positive momentum in equity markets over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1/INDPRO and SPX returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining residential construction (RSAFS) combined with high unemployment (UNRATE > 4.5%) signals weak demand conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where RSAFS declines and UNRATE exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects improved manufacturing capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO increases by > 1.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% monthly increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals tightening monetary policy that reduces equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises by 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 1,100k in a low-interest-rate environment (FEDFUNDS < 1.5%) indicates under-supplied housing markets, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST < 1,100k and FEDFUNDS < 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and rising Fed funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signal policy uncertainty that creates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL declines and FEDFUNDS rises.
